title: KnoTrust Dogfood
description: P0-E8-T2 dogfood dashboard for the P0-E8-T1 OTLP/HTTP audit-stream exporter (packages/otel), extended by R132 to cover the security-anomaly spans (fail_open_fired, denial_probing_suspected, tool_definition_changed, approval_channel_violation, probe_flagged). Every panel queries spans/attributes that packages/otel/src/span-mapper.ts's mapAuditEventToSpan actually emits — no invented attribute or metric names. See docs/03-engineering/dashboards/README.md for the full attribute-to-panel mapping and the SigNoz-version assumption.

service_name.name: service_name
service_name.description: OTel resource service.name to scope every panel to. Defaults to ALL (no filter) so the dashboard renders regardless of the exporter's configured telemetryExport.serviceName (default "knotrust" — packages/store/src/config.ts's TelemetryExportConfigSchema). Narrow it once you know your deployment's configured name.

widgets.title: Decisions per minute, by outcome
widgets.description: One series per knotrust.outcome value (allow | deny | pending_approval | deferred_not_eligible — the four stable Outcome literals from @knotrust/core's contract.ts), counting knotrust.decision spans over time. Source: packages/otel/src/span-mapper.ts's mapDecisionEvent — one span emitted per audited AuditEventType.DECISION event, named DECISION_SPAN_NAME ('knotrust.decision').

widgets.title: Added latency (ms) by path — p50 / p95 / p99
widgets.description: The decision span carries no single 'path' attribute, so path is DERIVED from two real attributes: knotrust.cache_hit (bool) and knotrust.outcome (string) — grouped here so every row is a real, queryable combination. Reading the rows: knotrust.cache_hit=true is the CACHE-HIT path. knotrust.cache_hit=false with knotrust.outcome NOT 'pending_approval' is the FRESH-EVALUATION path — this conflates L0 with any deeper PDP layer (cedar/opa/authzen_http/grant), because span-mapper.ts does not export an 'evaluatedBy' attribute: that field exists only on @knotrust/core's DecisionResponse and is never persisted to the audit log or the exported span (checked: packages/store/src/audit-log.ts's AuditEvent interface has no evaluatedBy field). knotrust.outcome='pending_approval' is the APPROVAL path's synchronous decision latency only (the time to produce the 'needs approval' decision) — it is NOT the end-to-end approval wait time; see the 'Approval resolution time' panel below for that. Values are knotrust.latency_ms, the exporter's own computed latency attribute.

aggregations.expression: p50(knotrust.latency_ms) as 'p50_ms' p95(knotrust.latency_ms) as 'p95_ms' p99(knotrust.latency_ms) as 'p99_ms' count() as 'n'

widgets.title: Decision latency p95 (ms) trend, cache-hit vs fresh-eval
widgets.description: Trend companion to the table above, using the one real boolean attribute that cleanly splits the cache-hit path from everything else: knotrust.cache_hit. p95(knotrust.latency_ms), grouped by knotrust.cache_hit, over knotrust.decision spans.

widgets.title: Denial reasons
widgets.description: Count of knotrust.decision spans where knotrust.outcome = 'deny', grouped by knotrust.reason (the decider's free-text reason code, e.g. no_grant_sensitive).

filter.expression: name = 'knotrust.decision' AND knotrust.outcome = 'deny' AND service.name in $service_name

widgets.title: Fail-open firings
widgets.description: Count of knotrust.security.fail_open_fired spans (R132) — one per AuditEventType.FAIL_OPEN_FIRED event appended by packages/proxy-stdio/src/enforce.ts's tryAppendFailOpenFired, mapped to a span by packages/otel/src/span-mapper.ts's mapSecurityAnomalyEvent. Grouped by knotrust.tier (always 'routine' — R84's fail-open is structurally routine-only) so a non-routine value here would itself be a red flag. knotrust.reason is a FIXED safe label ('fail_open_recovery'), never the event's own free-text cause (see span-mapper.ts's FAIL_OPEN_SAFE_REASON doc comment for why that text is deliberately never exported). Alert threshold: alert if this panel's value is > 0, since any fail-open firing is unexpected/notable by design (R84).

widgets.title: Approval resolution time (ms) — p50 / p95 / p99
widgets.description: Approval spans are point-in-time markers, not durations: mapApprovalEvent (span-mapper.ts) sets startTimeMs === endTimeMs === the event's own ts, so a single approval span's OWN duration is always zero — there is no latency_ms attribute on approval spans at all. The real 'approval resolution time' is the wall-clock gap between the requested/pending phase and the terminal phase (approved | denied | expired | cancelled) of the SAME knotrust.approval_id, which requires joining across the standalone per-phase spans (knotrust.approval.<phase>, see APPROVAL_SPAN_NAME_PREFIX in span-mapper.ts) — not expressible in the Query Builder's single-query aggregation model, hence ClickHouse SQL here (per SigNoz's own widget guidance: use ClickHouse SQL for cross-row/cross-group time-diff computations). CAVEAT (owner-run validation required, ruling R2): this SQL has been checked against the reference SigNoz MCP server's own published traces ClickHouse schema (signoz_traces.distributed_signoz_index_v3) and its 'Latency Between Spans in Trace' pattern, but has NOT been executed against a live SigNoz + real dogfood traffic in this environment — verify column/table names still match on import and adjust if your SigNoz version's schema has moved on.

clickhouse_sql.name: A
clickhouse_sql.query:
WITH approval_pairs AS (
  SELECT
    attributes_string['knotrust.approval_id'] AS approval_id,
    minIf(timestamp, name IN ('knotrust.approval.requested', 'knotrust.approval.pending')) AS requested_ts,
    maxIf(timestamp, name IN ('knotrust.approval.approved', 'knotrust.approval.denied', 'knotrust.approval.expired', 'knotrust.approval.cancelled')) AS terminal_ts
  FROM signoz_traces.distributed_signoz_index_v3
  WHERE name LIKE 'knotrust.approval.%'
    AND attributes_string['knotrust.approval_id'] != ''
    AND ts_bucket_start BETWEEN $start_timestamp - 1800 AND $end_timestamp
    AND timestamp BETWEEN {{.start_datetime}} AND {{.end_datetime}}
  GROUP BY approval_id
  HAVING requested_ts != toDateTime64(0, 9) AND terminal_ts != toDateTime64(0, 9) AND terminal_ts > requested_ts
),
resolved AS (
  SELECT
    toStartOfInterval(terminal_ts, INTERVAL 5 MINUTE) AS interval,
    toFloat64(toUnixTimestamp64Nano(terminal_ts) - toUnixTimestamp64Nano(requested_ts)) / 1000000 AS resolution_ms
  FROM approval_pairs
)
SELECT interval AS ts, 'p50' AS quantile, quantile(0.5)(resolution_ms) AS value FROM resolved GROUP BY interval
UNION ALL
SELECT interval AS ts, 'p95' AS quantile, quantile(0.95)(resolution_ms) AS value FROM resolved GROUP BY interval
UNION ALL
SELECT interval AS ts, 'p99' AS quantile, quantile(0.99)(resolution_ms) AS value FROM resolved GROUP BY interval
ORDER BY ts ASC, quantile ASC
